task_completion.description: Open the lung.csv dataset in JASP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP application window is open, displaying the data view with columns such as 'time', 'status', and 'sex' from the lung dataset.
The Kaplan-Meier analysis control panel is visible on the left side of the JASP interface.

task_completion.description: Assign 'time' to the Time variable and 'status' to the Event variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Kaplan-Meier control panel, the variable 'time' is placed in the 'Time' box, and the variable 'status' is placed in the 'Event' box.
In the Kaplan-Meier control panel, the variable 'sex' is placed in the 'Strata' box.
In the Kaplan-Meier control panel, the 'Event level' or 'Event indicates' dropdown/input field is explicitly set to '2'.

task_completion.description: Save the completed analysis as LungSurvival.jasp
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved as 'LungSurvival.jasp' in the ~/Documents/JASP/ directory, or the main JASP window title bar displays 'LungSurvival.jasp'.

integrity.description: The agent used the JASP graphical user interface to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the JASP application windows, menus, and dialogs, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The analysis produced genuine results within the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The right-side results pane in JASP displays actual Kaplan-Meier survival tables or plots corresponding to the input variables.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by using a script to generate the .jasp archive directly
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a clear progression of GUI steps (loading data, setting variables, saving) rather than a sudden appearance of the final .jasp file via a terminal script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be saved at /home/ga/Documents/JASP/LungSurvival.jasp. The analysis type must be Kaplan-Meier. The required variable mapping is: 'time' -> Time, 'status' -> Event, and 'sex' -> Strata. The event level indicating the event/death must be explicitly set to '2'.
